hypothesis: Si-based alloy anodes that maintain an invariant largest Li+ diffusion channel radius across lithiation and delithiation (Rt variation ≤ 0.15 Å) will exhibit substantially reduced SEI growth–driven lithium consumption and at least a twofold increase in cycle life at 1C in full Li-ion cells compared with Si–X alloys that do not preserve Rt, demonstrating that channel-geometry invariance directly enhances practical cyclability.
